current_deliverable: C111 complete: igt-wwpdb-analog-architecture.md written — R3 gap architecture specification for DC#3 full as AI Circuit Database (AICD). Network spine §9 updated with R1/R3 gap structure, ZR extended to C102–C111.
progress: C111 complete. AICD is the wwPDB analog for AI circuit claims. Five-property design: mandatory deposition (journal enforcement), apparatus-computed validation (automated, non-negotiable), consortium governance (independent of AI labs), discrepancy visibility (public failed-validation records), career structure for failure detection (adversarial review program). Three-phase cryo-EM timeline: Phase 1 (current, high OA-DoF) → Phase 2 (R1a achieved, voluntary deposition) → Phase 3 (R1 full + R3 full = DC#3 full, near-zero OA-DoF). Self-referential constraint formalized: AICD must itself be IGE-compliant. Spine §9 now has detailed R1/R3 gap structure with document cross-references.
remaining: P1 operationalization: detailed experimental design for circuit vs. behavioral replication — what constitutes cross-lab replication for circuit-level claims; what comparison cohort for behavioral benchmarks; timeline and measurability

R1 operationalization: concrete specification of what a 'TMS for AI circuits' (reference computation) would actually look like — candidate: modular arithmetic circuit; what properties make it suitable as the universal calibration anchor
MCCF per-instantiation confidence: IGE-MCCF eliminates independence uncertainty for physically-orthogonal instantiations — formal note needed
